articles.title: THE PEOPLE DO NOT YEARN FOR AUTOMATION
articles.summary: The article explores 'software brain,' a worldview that reduces everything to algorithms and databases. It discusses how this perspective, while powerful in creating the modern world, may not align with what people actually want.

articles.title: You’re about to feel the AI money squeeze
articles.summary: Anthropic restricted OpenClaw users and imposed costly payments for Claude AI access to reduce system strain and increase profitability. AI companies are monetizing their services as pressure mounts to become financially sustainable.

articles.title: Microsoft launches ‘vibe working’ in Word, Excel, and PowerPoint
articles.summary: Microsoft launches Agent Mode in Office apps, enabling AI to autonomously control Word, Excel, and PowerPoint. This enhanced Copilot experience, previously called 'vibe working,' offers more powerful automation for business users.

articles.title: Era raises $11M to build a software platform for AI gadgets
articles.summary: Era raised $11M to develop a software platform for diverse AI hardware devices like glasses, rings, and pendants, anticipating varied form factors in the future.

articles.title: Grab a ticket today: The first StrictlyVC of 2026 kicks off in just a week in San Francisco
articles.summary: StrictlyVC San Francisco event happens April 30 at Sentro Filipino Cultural Center. VCs and founders gather for networking and discussions.

articles.title: Another customer of troubled startup Delve suffered a big security incident
articles.summary: Delve, a troubled compliance startup, certified Context AI before the AI training company suffered a major security breach. This marks another incident involving Delve's certifications.

articles.title: Railway secures $100 million to challenge AWS with AI-native cloud infrastructure
articles.summary: Railway raised $100 million in Series B funding to build AI-native cloud infrastructure as an alternative to AWS and Google Cloud. The platform has attracted two million developers by addressing limitations of legacy cloud infrastructure for AI applications.

articles.title: Claude Code costs up to $200 a month. Goose does the same thing for free.
articles.summary: Claude Code charges $20-$200 monthly for AI coding, prompting developers to switch to Goose, a free open-source alternative offering similar functionality without subscription fees or cloud dependency.

articles.title: Listen Labs raises $69M after viral billboard hiring stunt to scale AI customer interviews
articles.summary: Listen Labs raised $69M Series B after a viral billboard campaign featuring AI tokens and a coding challenge helped recruit engineers. The creative hiring stunt decoded to a Berghain bouncer algorithm puzzle that attracted thousands of applicants.

articles.title: Microsoft issues emergency update for macOS and Linux ASP.NET threat
articles.summary: Microsoft released an emergency patch for ASP.NET Core to fix a high-severity vulnerability allowing unauthenticated attackers to gain SYSTEM privileges on Linux and macOS devices through faulty cryptographic signature verification.

articles.title: Contrary to popular superstition, AES 128 is just fine in a post-quantum world
articles.summary: Cryptography engineer Filippo Valsorda clarifies that AES 128 remains secure in a post-quantum world, contrary to common misconceptions. The encryption standard requires infeasible brute-force attacks and maintains no known vulnerabilities.

articles.title: US-sanctioned currency exchange says $15 million heist done by "unfriendly states"
articles.summary: Grinex, a US-sanctioned crypto exchange in Kyrgyzstan, shut down after a $15 million heist allegedly by state-sponsored hackers targeting Russian users, blaming 'unfriendly states' for the attack.

articles.title: The Download: introducing the Nature issue
articles.summary: Technology Review explores how human influence has permeated every corner of Earth, from microplastics in rainforests to artificial light in oceans, questioning what nature means today and technology's role in restoration.

articles.title: Will fusion power get cheap? Don’t count on it.
articles.summary: A new study questions whether fusion power will become affordable despite being a clean energy source. Unlike batteries and solar, fusion costs may not decline as rapidly over time.

articles.title: The Download: introducing the 10 Things That Matter in AI Right Now
articles.summary: MIT Technology Review created a guide of 10 Things That Matter in AI to help readers navigate hype and constant launches. The guide distills years of analysis to identify what truly matters in the rapidly evolving AI landscape.

articles.title: Gemma 4 VLA Demo on Jetson Orin Nano Super
articles.summary: Gemma 4 VLA (Vision Language Model) was demonstrated running on NVIDIA's Jetson Orin Nano Super, showcasing efficient edge AI capabilities for vision and language tasks on compact hardware.

articles.title: QIMMA قِمّة ⛰: A Quality-First Arabic LLM Leaderboard
articles.summary: QIMMA introduces a quality-focused leaderboard for evaluating Arabic Large Language Models, prioritizing benchmark quality over quantity to better assess LLM performance in Arabic language tasks.

articles.title: AI and the Future of Cybersecurity: Why Openness Matters
articles.summary: Openness in AI development is crucial for cybersecurity's future. Transparent practices help identify vulnerabilities and build trustworthy systems that protect against evolving threats.

articles.title: A conversation with Kevin Scott: What’s next in AI
articles.summary: Kevin Scott discusses the future direction of AI technology and its upcoming developments in a conversation featured on Microsoft's AI blog.

articles.title: From Hot Wheels to handling content: How brands are using Microsoft AI to be more productive and imaginative
articles.summary: Brands like Hot Wheels leverage Microsoft AI tools to enhance productivity and creativity in content creation and marketing. These solutions enable companies to work more efficiently while maintaining imaginative output.

articles.title: Microsoft open sources its ‘farm of the future’ toolkit
articles.summary: Microsoft open sources its 'farm of the future' toolkit, providing developers with tools to modernize agricultural practices using AI and technology.

articles.title: Elevating Austria: Google invests in its first data center in the Alps.
articles.summary: Google announced its first data center in Kronstorf, Austria, creating 100 direct jobs. The facility marks a significant investment in the Alpine region.

articles.title: We're launching two specialized TPUs for the agentic era.
articles.summary: Google launches two specialized TPUs as part of its eighth generation chips designed to power the future of AI in the agentic era.

articles.title: 3 new ways Ads Advisor is making Google Ads safer and faster
articles.summary: Google Ads Advisor introduces three new agentic safety and policy features to protect and streamline Google Ads accounts, enhancing security and efficiency for advertisers.

articles.title: Decoupled DiLoCo: A new frontier for resilient, distributed AI training
articles.summary: Decoupled DiLoCo introduces a new method for distributed AI training that improves resilience and efficiency. The approach decouples local and global optimization steps, enabling more robust training across distributed systems.

articles.title: Partnering with industry leaders to accelerate AI transformation
articles.summary: Google DeepMind partners with global consultancies to help organizations worldwide implement frontier AI technologies. This collaboration aims to accelerate AI transformation across industries.

articles.title: Gemini 3.1 Flash TTS: the next generation of expressive AI speech
articles.summary: Google releases Gemini 3.1 Flash TTS, an advanced audio model featuring granular audio tags for precise control over expressive AI-generated speech.

articles.title: LWiAI Podcast #238 - GPT 5.4 mini, OpenAI Pivot, Mamba 3, Attention Residuals
articles.summary: OpenAI released GPT-5.4 mini and nano models with 400k-token context windows and improved token efficiency. Mistral open-sourced Small 4 model family while the podcast discusses Mamba 3 and attention residuals.

articles.title: Last Week in AI #339 - DLSS 5, OpenAI Superapp, MiniMax M2.7
articles.summary: Nvidia released DLSS 5, a generative AI technology that enhances video game graphics in real-time, improving photorealism. The technology has potential applications beyond gaming.

articles.title: LWiAI Podcast #237 - Nemotron 3 Super, xAI reborn, Anthropic Lawsuit, Research!
articles.summary: Episode 237 discusses major AI developments including Perplexity's local Mac-based AI agent, Anthropic's GitHub PR pricing, Cursor's coding automations, and new interactive visualization features from ChatGPT and Anthropic.

articles.title: The billion-dollar startup with a different idea for AI
articles.summary: Yann LeCun's AMI Labs received $1 billion in funding to pursue an alternative AI approach using modular, task-specific components rather than large language models, with no expected product for five years.

articles.title: NVIDIA and Google infrastructure cuts AI inference costs
articles.summary: Google and NVIDIA unveiled A5X bare-metal instances using NVIDIA Vera Rubin GPUs and Google Virgo networking to reduce AI inference costs by 10x while improving token throughput per megawatt.

articles.title: Sony AI robot beats players as humanoid robot wins Beijing race
articles.summary: Sony AI's robot Ace defeated high-level human table tennis players in regulated matches, demonstrating physical AI capabilities through rapid perception and precise motor control in competitive environments.

articles.title: Which Agent Causes Task Failures and When?Researchers from PSU and Duke explores automated failure attribution of LLM Multi-Agent Systems
articles.summary: Researchers from PSU and Duke developed methods to automatically identify which agent causes task failures in LLM multi-agent systems and when failures occur, improving collaborative AI debugging.

articles.title: ByteDance Introduces Astra: A Dual-Model Architecture for Autonomous Robot Navigation
articles.summary: ByteDance introduced Astra, a dual-model architecture for autonomous robot navigation that addresses key challenges like localization, self-positioning, and path planning in complex indoor environments.

articles.title: MIT Researchers Unveil “SEAL”: A New Step Towards Self-Improving AI
articles.summary: MIT researchers introduced SEAL, a framework enabling large language models to self-improve by generating training data and updating their own weights through learned self-editing processes via reinforcement learning.

articles.title: Anthropic’s MCP vulnerability: When ‘expected behavior’ becomes a supply chain nightmare
articles.summary: Security researchers discovered a critical architectural flaw in Anthropic's Model Context Protocol that exposes millions of AI applications to remote takeover attacks, potentially affecting entire supply chains.

articles.title: The paradox of LLM self-distillation: Faster reasoning, weaker generalization
articles.summary: LLM self-distillation optimizes for speed but weakens generalization, reducing models' ability to explore alternative solutions on difficult problems.

articles.title: Why harness engineering is becoming the new AI moat
articles.summary: As large language models become commoditized, sophisticated engineering harnesses around them emerge as the true competitive advantage. The Anthropic Claude Code leak demonstrates that the infrastructure supporting AI models matters more than the models themselves.

articles.title: Want free e-books? Stuff Your Kindle Day has 150+ titles discounted - today only
articles.summary: Stuff Your Kindle Day offers over 150 discounted e-books available for free today. The promotion works on any e-reader device and purchased books are yours to keep permanently.

articles.title: Why I recommend this $450 Samsung phone over competing models by Google and OnePlus
articles.summary: The Galaxy A37, priced at $450, outperforms competing Google and OnePlus models with upgraded cameras. However, it faces significant competition in the midrange smartphone market.

articles.title: Apple just fixed an iOS flaw exploited by the FBI - here's what happened
articles.summary: Apple released iOS 26.4.2 to fix a security vulnerability that the FBI exploited to recover deleted Signal messages from users' devices.

articles.title: Building AI Agents with Local Small Language Models
articles.summary: Local small language models now enable developers to build AI agents independently, democratizing technology previously limited to large tech companies.

articles.title: Train, Serve, and Deploy a Scikit-learn Model with FastAPI
articles.summary: FastAPI is a popular framework for serving machine learning models due to its lightweight, fast, and user-friendly nature, enabling efficient training, deployment, and serving of Scikit-learn models.

articles.title: AI Agent Memory Explained in 3 Levels of Difficulty
articles.summary: The article explains AI agent memory across three difficulty levels, focusing on how stateless AI agents lack memory of previous interactions and calls.

articles.title: Using a Local LLM as a Zero-Shot Classifier
articles.summary: A practical pipeline demonstrates using locally hosted LLMs for zero-shot classification of unstructured text data into meaningful categories without requiring labeled training data.

articles.title: I Simulated an International Supply Chain and Let OpenClaw Monitor It
articles.summary: A live supply chain simulation was built and monitored by an AI agent to investigate why 18% of shipments were late despite all teams meeting targets.

articles.title: Your Synthetic Data Passed Every Test and Still Broke Your Model
articles.summary: Synthetic data can pass all tests but still fail in production due to hidden gaps. The article explores how these silent failures occur when models encounter real-world scenarios.

articles.title: Tag, You’re It: GeForce NOW Levels Up Game Discovery With Xbox Game Pass and Ubisoft+ Labels
articles.summary: GeForce NOW adds new in-app labels for Xbox Game Pass and Ubisoft+ games, improving game discovery and helping gamers find content more easily in their cloud libraries.

articles.title: Making Sense of the Early Universe
articles.summary: The article discusses methods for understanding the early universe's formation and characteristics. Scientists use advanced observations and theoretical models to interpret cosmic phenomena.

articles.title: From Rainforests to Recycling Plants: 5 Ways NVIDIA AI Is Protecting the Planet
articles.summary: NVIDIA AI technology is being deployed across environmental applications, from rainforest monitoring to optimizing recycling plants, helping protect ecosystems and improve sustainability efforts globally.

articles.title: Applying multimodal biological foundation models across therapeutics and patient care
articles.summary: Multimodal biological foundation models integrate fragmented healthcare data streams like genomics, medical images, and patient records to improve disease diagnosis, drug discovery, and treatment outcomes. AWS provides a unified platform for these models, enabling better personalized medicine decision-making.

articles.title: Cost-effective multilingual audio transcription at scale with Parakeet-TDT and AWS Batch
articles.summary: NVIDIA Parakeet-TDT model deployed on AWS Batch enables cost-effective multilingual audio transcription at scale. Its Token-and-Duration Transducer architecture achieves faster-than-real-time inference while reducing ASR costs significantly.

articles.title: Amazon SageMaker AI now supports optimized generative AI inference recommendations
articles.summary: Amazon SageMaker AI now offers optimized generative AI inference recommendations, providing validated deployment configurations and performance metrics to accelerate model production deployment and reduce infrastructure management overhead.

articles.title: Simplify Sparse Deep Learning with Universal Sparse Tensor in nvmath-python
articles.summary: NVIDIA introduced Universal Sparse Tensor (UST) in nvmath-python, decoupling tensor sparsity from memory layout for improved flexibility and performance in sparse deep learning applications.

articles.title: Scaling the AI-Ready Data Center with NVIDIA RTX PRO 4500 Blackwell Server Edition and NVIDIA vGPU 20
articles.summary: NVIDIA's RTX PRO 4500 Blackwell Server Edition enables AI-ready data centers for enterprise applications. The GPU supports diverse workloads from productivity software to complex design tools.

articles.title: Advancing Emerging Optimizers for Accelerated LLM Training with NVIDIA Megatron
articles.summary: NVIDIA Megatron advances higher-order optimization algorithms like Shampoo and Muon to accelerate LLM training. These methods improve neural network training efficiency for large language models.

articles.title: How generational differences affect consumer attitudes towards ads
articles.summary: A research study with CrowdDNA examines how different generations perceive and respond to social media advertisements across various platforms, revealing generational differences in consumer attitudes toward ads.

articles.title: Every tree counts
articles.summary: Meta aims for net zero emissions by 2030, developing carbon mitigation technology and making it publicly available to reduce environmental impact.

articles.title: How a non-traditional background led to cutting-edge XR tech
articles.summary: An individual with a non-traditional background developed innovative XR technology by bringing fresh perspectives and unconventional approaches to the field.

articles.title: Gradient-based Planning for World Models at Longer Horizons
articles.summary: GRASP is a gradient-based planner for world models enabling practical long-horizon planning through parallel trajectory optimization, stochastic exploration, and gradient reshaping for cleaner action signals.

articles.title: Identifying Interactions at Scale for LLMs
articles.summary: Researchers present methods for identifying interactions at scale in Large Language Models to improve interpretability. This work analyzes LLM behavior through feature attribution and interaction analysis for safer, more transparent AI systems.

articles.title: Information-Driven Design of Imaging Systems
articles.summary: Research on information-driven design of imaging systems that uses encoders to map objects to images and quantifies measurement quality through noise models and information estimation.
